label: BMS — Conexpro
info:
Subscribes to the conexpro-bms-bridge state topic (default `bms/main/state`),
stashes the full snapshot in `flow.bms_latest`, maps the dashboard-relevant
fields into `global.telemetry`, and writes time-series data to InfluxDB with
per-metric decimation:

  bms_live    — every tick (~30 s):  voltage_v, current_a, power_w, soc_pct,
                                     remaining_ah, *_mosfet, balance_active,
                                     protection_active
  bms_state   — every 5 min OR on significant change of cell delta /
                protection / balance state: cell_min/max/delta + indices
  bms_cells   — every 10 min OR on >5 mV change: per-cell voltage tagged
                with cell_idx (Grafana GROUP BY cell_idx)
  bms_temps   — every 10 min OR on >0.5 °C change: per-NTC temp tagged
                with ntc_idx
  bms_meta    — every hour: cycle_count, capacity_ah, cell_count, ntc_count

The write-decimating function uses flow context for last-write-at
timestamps. Disabling InfluxDB simply means deleting the bms_to_influx +
influx_out nodes; everything else (live MQTT consumption, dashboard
composer mapping) keeps working.

name: stash + map → global.telemetry
decimate → influx writes
track availability
